# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 7.2.1 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.2.1/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Link to this heading")

=====================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.2.1/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.2.1/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.2.1/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
    *   [Tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.2.1/tutorial/index.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.2.1/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.2.1/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.2.1/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.2.1/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Snippets](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.2.1/topics/snippets/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.2.1/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 6.3.5 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.3.5/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Link to this heading")

=====================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.3.5/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.3.5/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.3.5/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
    *   [Tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.3.5/tutorial/index.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.3.5/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.3.5/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.3.5/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.3.5/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Snippets](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.3.5/topics/snippets/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.3.5/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 7.1.2 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.1.2/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Link to this heading")

=====================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.1.2/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.1.2/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.1.2/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
    *   [Tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.1.2/tutorial/index.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.1.2/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.1.2/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.1.2/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.1.2/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Snippets](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.1.2/topics/snippets/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.1.2/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 1.1 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.1/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/v1.1/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.1/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

=========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.1/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.1/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.1/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Creating your Wagtail site**
    *   [Creating page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.1/topics/creating_pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.1/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [Images](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.1/topics/images/index.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.1/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.1/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.1/editor_manual/index.html)

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 0.7 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v0.7/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

=========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is a modern, flexible CMS, built on Django.

It supports Django 1.6.2+ and 1.7rc3+ on Python 2.6, 2.7, 3.2, 3.3 and 3.4.

*   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v0.7/getting_started/index.html)

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v0.5/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

=========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is a modern, flexible CMS, built on Django.

It supports Django 1.6.2+ on Python 2.6, 2.7, 3.2, 3.3 and 3.4. Django 1.7 support is in progress pending further release candidate testing.

*   [Getting Started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v0.5/gettingstarted.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 0.6 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v0.6/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/v0.6/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v0.6/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

=========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is a modern, flexible CMS, built on Django.

It supports Django 1.6.2+ and 1.7rc3+ on Python 2.6, 2.7, 3.2, 3.3 and 3.4.

*   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v0.6/getting_started/index.html)

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.4.6/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

===========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

*   **First steps**
    *   [_Getting started_](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.4.6/getting_started/index.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 0.8.10 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v0.8.10/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/v0.8.10/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v0.8.10/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

============================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is a modern, flexible CMS, built on Django.

It supports Django 1.6.2+ and 1.7.1+ on Python 2.6, 2.7, 3.2, 3.3 and 3.4.

*   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v0.8.10/getting_started/index.html)
    *   [Installation](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v0.8.10/getting_started/installation.html)
        
    *   [Creating your project](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v0.8.10/getting_started/creating_your_project.html)
        
*   [Core components](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v0.8.10/core_components/index.html)
    *   [Sites](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v0.8.10/core_components/sites.html)
        
    *   [Pages](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v0.8.10/core_components/pages/index.html)

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.6.3/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

===========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.8.2/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

===========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.9.1/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

===========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.9.1/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.9.1/getting_started/tutorial.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 1.10.1 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.10.1/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/d3b9531f5b97f317f9ed83ed0d6cd44404b03df1/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.10.1/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

============================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.10.1/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.10.1/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.10.1/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.10.1/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.10.1/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [Using images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.10.1/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.10.1/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.10.1/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.10.1/editor_manual/index.html)

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.11.1/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

============================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

.

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 1.13.4 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.13.4/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/v1.13.4/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.13.4/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

============================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.13.4/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.13.4/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.13.4/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.13.4/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.13.4/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [Using images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.13.4/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.13.4/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.13.4/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v1.13.4/editor_manual/index.html)

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.0.2/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

===========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

*   **First steps**

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 2.1.3 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.1.3/#)

*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.1.3/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

===========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, [explore the Wagtail Bakery](https://docs.wagtail.org/divio.com/wagtail)
, a fully-functional interactive demo (hosted at Divio Cloud).

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.1.3/getting_started/index.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 2.2.2 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.2.2/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/v2.2.2/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.2.2/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

===========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, [explore the Wagtail Bakery](https://docs.wagtail.org/divio.com/wagtail)
, a fully-functional interactive demo (hosted at Divio Cloud).

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.2.2/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.2.2/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.2.2/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.2.2/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.2.2/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [Using images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.2.2/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.2.2/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.2.2/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.2.2/editor_manual/index.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 2.3 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.3/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/v2.3/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.3/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

=========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, [explore the Wagtail Bakery](https://divio.com/wagtail)
, a fully-functional interactive demo (hosted at Divio Cloud).

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.3/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.3/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.3/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.3/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.3/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [Using images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.3/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.3/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.3/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.3/editor_manual/index.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 2.5.2 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.5.2/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/v2.5.2/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.5.2/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

===========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, [explore the Wagtail Bakery](https://divio.com/wagtail)
, a fully-functional interactive demo (hosted at Divio Cloud).

*   **First steps**

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 2.4 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.4/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/v2.4/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.4/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

=========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, [explore the Wagtail Bakery](https://divio.com/wagtail)
, a fully-functional interactive demo (hosted at Divio Cloud).

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.4/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.4/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.4/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.4/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.4/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [Using images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.4/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.4/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.4/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.4/editor_manual/index.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 2.6.3 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.6.3/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/v2.6.3/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.6.3/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

===========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, [explore the Wagtail Bakery](https://divio.com/wagtail)
, a fully-functional interactive demo (hosted at Divio Cloud).

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.6.3/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.6.3/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.6.3/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.6.3/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.6.3/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [Using images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.6.3/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.6.3/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.6.3/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.6.3/editor_manual/index.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 2.7.1 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.7.1/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/v2.7.1/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.7.1/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

===========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, [explore the Wagtail Bakery](https://divio.com/wagtail)
, a fully-functional interactive demo (hosted at Divio Cloud).

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.7.1/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.7.1/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.7.1/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.7.1/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.7.1/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [Using images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.7.1/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.7.1/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.7.1/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.7.1/editor_manual/index.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 2.8.2 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.8.2/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/v2.8.2/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.8.2/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

===========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, [explore the Wagtail Bakery](https://divio.com/wagtail)
, a fully-functional interactive demo (hosted at Divio Cloud).

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.8.2/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.8.2/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.8.2/getting_started/demo_site.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 2.9.3 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.9.3/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/v2.9.3/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.9.3/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

===========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, [explore the Wagtail Bakery](https://divio.com/wagtail)
, a fully-functional interactive demo (hosted at Divio Cloud).

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.9.3/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.9.3/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.9.3/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.9.3/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.9.3/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [Using images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.9.3/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.9.3/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.9.3/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.9.3/editor_manual/index.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 2.10.2 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.10.2/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/v2.10.2/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.10.2/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

============================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, [explore the Wagtail Bakery](https://divio.com/wagtail)
, a fully-functional interactive demo (hosted at Divio Cloud).

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.10.2/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.10.2/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.10.2/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.10.2/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.10.2/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [Using images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.10.2/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.10.2/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.10.2/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.10.2/editor_manual/index.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 2.11.8 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.11.8/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/v2.11.8/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.11.8/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

============================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, [explore the Wagtail Bakery](https://divio.com/wagtail)
, a fully-functional interactive demo (hosted at Divio Cloud).

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.11.8/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.11.8/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.11.8/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.11.8/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.11.8/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [Using images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.11.8/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.11.8/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.11.8/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.11.8/editor_manual/index.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail 2.12.5 documentation

*   [Docs](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.12.5/#)
     »
*   Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/v2.12.5/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.12.5/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

============================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, [explore the Wagtail Bakery](https://divio.com/wagtail)
, a fully-functional interactive demo (hosted at Divio Cloud).

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.12.5/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.12.5/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.12.5/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.12.5/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.12.5/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [Using images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.12.5/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.12.5/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.12.5/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.12.5/editor_manual/index.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 2.13.5 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.13.5/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

============================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.io/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.13.5/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.13.5/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.13.5/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.13.5/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.13.5/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.13.5/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.13.5/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.13.5/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.13.5/editor_manual/index.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 2.14.2 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.14.2/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

============================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.io/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.14.2/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.14.2/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.14.2/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.14.2/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.14.2/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.14.2/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.14.2/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.14.2/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.14.2/editor_manual/index.html)
        

Index[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.14.2/#index "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.14.2/getting_started/index.html)
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.14.2/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.14.2/getting_started/demo_site.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 4.1.9 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.1.9/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this heading")

==========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.1.9/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.1.9/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.1.9/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.1.9/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.1.9/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.1.9/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.1.9/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.1.9/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/4.1.x/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 4.2.4 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.2.4/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this heading")

==========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.2.4/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.2.4/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.2.4/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.2.4/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.2.4/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.2.4/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.2.4/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.2.4/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/4.2.x/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 7.2.1 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/index.html#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Link to this heading")

===============================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
    *   [Tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/tutorial/index.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Snippets](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/topics/snippets/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)
        

Index[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/index.html#index "Link to this heading")

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 2.15.6 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.15.6/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

============================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.io/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.15.6/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.15.6/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.15.6/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.15.6/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.15.6/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.15.6/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.15.6/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.15.6/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.15.6/editor_manual/index.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 5.0.5 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.0.5/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this heading")

==========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.0.5/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.0.5/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.0.5/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.0.5/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.0.5/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.0.5/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.0.5/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.0.5/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 2.16.3 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.16.3/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

============================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.16.3/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.16.3/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.16.3/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.16.3/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.16.3/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.16.3/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.16.3/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.16.3/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.16.3/editor_manual/index.html)
        

Index[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v2.16.3/#index "Permalink to this headline")

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 3.0.3 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v3.0.3/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

===========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v3.0.3/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v3.0.3/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v3.0.3/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v3.0.3/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v3.0.3/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v3.0.3/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v3.0.3/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v3.0.3/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v3.0.3/editor_manual/index.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 4.0.4 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.0.4/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

===========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.0.4/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.0.4/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.0.4/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.0.4/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.0.4/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.0.4/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.0.4/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.0.4/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v4.0.4/editor_manual/index.html)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 5.1.3 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.1.3/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this heading")

==========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.1.3/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.1.3/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.1.3/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.1.3/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.1.3/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.1.3/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.1.3/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Snippets](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.1.3/topics/snippets/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.1.3/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 5.2.8 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.2.8/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Permalink to this heading")

==========================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.2.8/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.2.8/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.2.8/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.2.8/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.2.8/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.2.8/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.2.8/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Snippets](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.2.8/topics/snippets/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v5.2.8/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 6.0.6 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.0.6/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Link to this heading")

=====================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.0.6/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.0.6/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.0.6/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
    *   [Tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.0.6/tutorial/index.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.0.6/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.0.6/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.0.6/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.0.6/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Snippets](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.0.6/topics/snippets/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.0.6/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 6.1.3 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.1.3/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Link to this heading")

=====================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.1.3/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.1.3/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.1.3/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
    *   [Tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.1.3/tutorial/index.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.1.3/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.1.3/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.1.3/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.1.3/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Snippets](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.1.3/topics/snippets/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.1.3/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 6.2.4 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.2.4/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Link to this heading")

=====================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.2.4/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.2.4/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.2.4/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
    *   [Tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.2.4/tutorial/index.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.2.4/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.2.4/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.2.4/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.2.4/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Snippets](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.2.4/topics/snippets/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.2.4/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 7.3a0 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/latest/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Link to this heading")

=====================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/latest/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/latest/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/latest/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
    *   [Tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/latest/tutorial/index.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/latest/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/latest/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/latest/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/latest/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Snippets](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/latest/topics/snippets/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/latest/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 6.4.2 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.4.2/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Link to this heading")

=====================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.4.2/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.4.2/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.4.2/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
    *   [Tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.4.2/tutorial/index.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.4.2/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.4.2/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.4.2/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.4.2/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Snippets](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.4.2/topics/snippets/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v6.4.2/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 7.0.3 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.0.3/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Link to this heading")

=====================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.0.3/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.0.3/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.0.3/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
    *   [Tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.0.3/tutorial/index.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.0.3/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.0.3/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.0.3/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.0.3/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Snippets](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.0.3/topics/snippets/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/v7.0.3/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 6.3.5 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.3/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Link to this heading")

==================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.3/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.3/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.3/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
    *   [Tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.3/tutorial/index.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.3/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.3/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.3/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.3/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Snippets](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.3/topics/snippets/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.3/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 6.4.2 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.4/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Link to this heading")

==================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.4/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.4/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.4/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
    *   [Tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.4/tutorial/index.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.4/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.4/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.4/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.4/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Snippets](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.4/topics/snippets/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/6.4/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)

# Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation — Wagtail Documentation 7.0.3 documentation

* * *

Welcome to Wagtail’s documentation[¶](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/7.0/#welcome-to-wagtail-s-documentation "Link to this heading")

==================================================================================================================================

Wagtail is an open source CMS written in [Python](https://www.python.org/)
 and built on the [Django web framework](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
.

Below are some useful links to help you get started with Wagtail.

If you’d like to get a quick feel for Wagtail, try spinning up a [temporary developer environment](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail-gitpod)
 in your browser (running on Gitpod - here’s [how it works](https://wagtail.org/blog/gitpod/)
).

*   **First steps**
    
    *   [Getting started](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/7.0/getting_started/index.html)
        
    *   [Your first Wagtail site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/7.0/getting_started/tutorial.html)
        
    *   [Demo site](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/7.0/getting_started/demo_site.html)
        
    *   [Tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/7.0/tutorial/index.html)
        
*   **Using Wagtail**
    
    *   [Page models](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/7.0/topics/pages.html)
        
    *   [Writing templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/7.0/topics/writing_templates.html)
        
    *   [How to use images in templates](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/7.0/topics/images.html)
        
    *   [Search](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/7.0/topics/search/index.html)
        
    *   [Snippets](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/7.0/topics/snippets/index.html)
        
    *   [Third-party tutorials](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/7.0/advanced_topics/third_party_tutorials.html)
        
*   **For editors**
    
    *   [Editors guide (separate site)](https://guide.wagtail.org/)
